linting.notes: Go linting uses golangci-lint without a config file (uses defaults). Python client uses ruff and mypy via nox sessions. Pre-commit hooks are configured for both.

testing.mock_patterns: Uses testcontainers-go for database integration tests
testing.notes: Most tests require testcontainers (Docker) to run MySQL/PostgreSQL databases. Pure unit tests (e.g., internal/converter) work without external dependencies. Python tests require Kubernetes clusters and are run via nox.

gating_checks.name: prepare
gating_checks.description: Runs code generation, go vet, and linting. Ensures no uncommitted changes.

gating_checks.name: build
gating_checks.command: make build/compile && make test-cover && make -C catalog test-cover
gating_checks.description: Compiles Go code and runs unit tests with coverage for main and catalog.

gating_checks.name: python-lint
gating_checks.description: Runs ruff and mypy on Python client.

gating_checks.name: python-e2e
gating_checks.description: Runs Python E2E tests against Kubernetes cluster with model-registry deployed.

gating_checks.name: controller-test
gating_checks.description: Runs controller tests (only when controller paths change).

advisory_checks.name: check-autogen
advisory_checks.command: cd clients/python && make clean install tidy
advisory_checks.description: Verifies Python autogenerated code is in sync.

ci.notes: CI runs on pull_request events. Most workflows skip on doc/config file changes. Python E2E tests require Kubernetes (kind cluster), model-registry deployment, and Minio. Controller and CSI tests only run when relevant paths change.

build.notes: Full build requires: (1) make deps to install tools (golangci-lint, goverter, openapi-generator-cli), (2) make gen to generate code (requires npm for openapi-generator), (3) make vet for go vet, (4) make lint for golangci-lint, (5) go build -buildvcs=false to compile. Simplified build: go build -buildvcs=false works if generated code already exists.

conventions.test_naming: Go: TestFunctionName(t *testing.T), Python: test_function_name() or @pytest.mark decorators
conventions.import_style: Go: grouped imports (standard, external, internal). Python: absolute imports from model_registry package.
conventions.notes: Go tests use testcontainers pattern for integration tests. Python uses pytest with nox for test orchestration. E2E tests require full infrastructure (Kubernetes, databases, Minio).

test_commands.notes: Requires testcontainers (MySQL/PostgreSQL containers). Fails with 'rootless Docker not found' in simple container.

container_recipe.notes: Most integration tests require testcontainers-go which needs Docker socket access. Pure unit tests (e.g., internal/converter) work in a standard container. For full test validation, use Docker-in-Docker or bind-mount Docker socket.

results.output_snippet:
panic: rootless Docker not found

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go/internal/core.MustExtractDockerHost.func1()
results.notes: Integration tests require testcontainers (Docker). Cannot run without Docker socket access.

validation.summary: Install OK, vet OK, lint OK (0 issues), build OK (34M binary), unit tests OK (converter). Integration tests require Docker/testcontainers.

gaps: Most Go tests require testcontainers (MySQL/PostgreSQL) and cannot run in a simple container without Docker socket access
Python tests require Kubernetes cluster (kind), model-registry deployment, Minio, and OCI registry for E2E testing
Controller tests require Kubernetes environment with KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS
CSI tests require Kubernetes cluster and Helm
Full build requires code generation (make gen) which needs npm and openapi-generator-cli - not validated in container
No root .golangci.yaml config file - golangci-lint uses defaults
Python client has separate nox-based test infrastructure not validated in Go container
